title: Clean Command Center UI, Collapsed Layers Default, Parcel Intelligence Flow, and Snapshot Library
scope: Frontend UX/product organization pass for Overview, layer rail behavior, selected parcel intelligence, and Planning Snapshot local library.

command_center_redesign.old_issue: The Phase 22D command cards still behaved like cramped cards and could overflow under the right intelligence panel at desktop width.
command_center_redesign.new_design: Replaced the card layout with a compact command action bar under the CFS Command Center heading.
readability_changes: Removed long paragraphs from action buttons.
Kept helper text below the command bar instead of inside squeezed cards.
Used a contained two-column action grid until very wide screens to prevent overlap with the right panel.
Browser smoke found and fixed a real overlap bug where command buttons were visually underneath the Intelligence panel.

collapsed_layer_rail_behavior.default_state: Overview starts with the left Layers rail collapsed/slim.
collapsed_layer_rail_behavior.countywide_intelligence_action: Dispatches a layer-rail open event so the advanced map controls expand on demand.

parcel_search_showcase_flow.search_action: Search Parcel focuses the top parcel search input.
parcel_search_showcase_flow.selected_parcel_brief: When a parcel is selected, the right panel uses the title Selected Parcel Intelligence.

countywide_intelligence_behavior.right_panel: If no parcel is selected, the right panel shows Intelligence Brief with countywide headline stats and safe model status.
countywide_intelligence_behavior.selected_parcel_override: If a parcel is selected and Countywide Intelligence is clicked, the right panel temporarily shows countywide stats without dropping the selected parcel.
countywide_intelligence_behavior.return_action: A Show Selected Parcel Intelligence action returns the right panel to the selected parcel brief.

snapshot_object_storage_design.storage: Frontend localStorage/state only; no backend or PostGIS persistence.

library_shape.savedSnapshots: Stored as a bounded local snapshot array.
library_shape.activeSnapshotId: Tracks which snapshot drives the report builder.

snapshot_object_storage_design.backward_compatibility: Legacy phase22a_v1 and phase22b_v1 snapshots still load and migrate into the library path when available.

multiple_snapshot_library_behavior.location: Planning Snapshot page, above the Snapshot workflow tabs.
visible_fields: selected parcel ID or map/context only
multiple_snapshot_library_behavior.empty_state: Shows No planning snapshots saved yet and directs users back to Overview to click Save Snapshot.

map_screenshot_report_behavior.capture_method: Existing ArcGIS SceneView screenshot helper is called when Save Snapshot runs.
map_screenshot_report_behavior.success_behavior: Saved snapshot stores mapScreenshotDataUrl and shows thumbnail/library/report image.
map_screenshot_report_behavior.failure_behavior: Snapshot still saves with failed/unavailable status and report shows a clean placeholder; no fake screenshots or broken image icons.
map_screenshot_report_behavior.browser_result: Smoke test saved snapshots with captured map thumbnails and verified Executive Summary map section.

documentation_updates: README demo/product mode section now describes compact Command Center actions, collapsed layers, and snapshot library.
Demo walkthrough now includes Save Snapshot, Planning Snapshot Library, Use / Open, and Delete.
Final demo checklist now verifies collapsed Layers rail and snapshot library.
Two-minute script and higher-up speaking notes now describe multiple saved snapshots and advanced layer controls.

safety_verification.browser_ui_result: No forbidden parcel-level model terms appeared in Overview, Planning Snapshot, Executive Summary, or Methodology smoke checks.
safety_verification.model_wording: Overview and report wording uses internal model research / aggregate governance / internal only, and does not expose parcel probabilities or ranking classes.

validation_results.npm_run_dev_cfs: passed after clearing stale generated .next/dev cache caused by a prior interrupted dev run
validation_results.api_checks: passed via npm run dev:cfs launcher

smoke_test_results.selected_parcel_intelligence: passed in browser before final layout-only fix; final dev:cfs API parcel search/detail checks also passed
smoke_test_results.multiple_snapshots_show_in_library: passed, library showed 3 saved snapshots before delete
smoke_test_results.snapshot_delete: passed, library dropped to 2 saved snapshots

remaining_known_issues: Browser plugin text entry hit a virtual clipboard limitation during the final post-fix parcel search retry, so the final selected-parcel search was validated through the dev:cfs API checks and an earlier same-session browser parcel-selection smoke before the layout-only command bar fix.
Snapshot library is intentionally localStorage/frontend state only; database persistence is not included in Phase 22E.
Map screenshot capture depends on the browser/ArcGIS SceneView runtime and can still fall back to a clean unavailable placeholder.
recommended_next_step: Run one manual demo pass in the visible browser at localhost:3000 to confirm the subjective command-center feel at the exact presentation viewport.
